name: col
readme:
Short:        Filter reverse line feeds (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.20

col - Filter reverse line feeds (OpenBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  col [-bfhpx] [-l num]

  Filters output from nroff/tbl that contains reverse line feeds and
  backspace-overstrike sequences. Useful for converting man page output
  to plain text.

Options:
  -b          do not output backspace characters
  -f          forward half line feeds permitted
  -h          compress spaces to tabs on output
  -p          output unknown escape sequences
  -x          do not compress spaces to tabs
  -l num      buffer num lines (default 128)

Examples:
  nroff -man page.1 | col -b     ; strip overstrike from man page
  col -bx <formatted.txt         ; plain text, no tab compression

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Standard Tier 1 port. 29 tests cover plain pass-through, reverse line feed filtering, backspace-overstrike (-b), half-line feeds (-f), SI/SO charset markers, tab compression (-h/-x), -l buffer size, and stress tests with complex nroff-style formatting sequences.
